ThingDef.label: Sakin MG38 (Negev NG7)
ThingDef.description: A complex light machine gun design with mechanisms allowing a low but consistent firerate to control recoil, paired with a seemingly limitless ammo reservoir for situations requiring a continuous, non-stop output.

ThingDef.label: Sakin MG38 INF (Negev NG7)
ThingDef.description: A complex light machine gun design with mechanisms allowing a low but consistent firerate to control recoil, paired with a seemingly limitless ammo reservoir for situations requiring a continuous, non-stop output. INF stands for Infantry. This variant represents the standardise weapon used by a soldier. It's good in build and good at medium to long range.

ThingDef.label: Sakin MG38 CQC (Negev NG7)
ThingDef.description: A complex light machine gun design with mechanisms allowing a low but consistent firerate to control recoil, paired with a seemingly limitless ammo reservoir for situations requiring a continuous, non-stop output. CQC stands for Close Quaters Combat. This variant is designed for upclose encounters like room clearing and urban warfare. Sacrifices long range for better short range accuracy.

ThingDef.label: Sakin MG38 SF (Negev NG7)
ThingDef.description: A complex light machine gun design with mechanisms allowing a low but consistent firerate to control recoil, paired with a seemingly limitless ammo reservoir for situations requiring a continuous, non-stop output. SF stands for Special Forces. This variant is equipped with a supressor and more quality teched gear. Perfect for anyone who needs to be stealthy and efficient.

ThingDef.label: Sakin MG38 THM (Negev NG7)
ThingDef.description: A complex light machine gun design with mechanisms allowing a low but consistent firerate to control recoil, paired with a seemingly limitless ammo reservoir for situations requiring a continuous, non-stop output. THM stands for Thermals. This variant is equipped with a Thermal Scope. Being able to see your target lit up like a Christmas Tree makes target acquisition easier. However, when the target gets closer it becomes harder to see.

ThingDef.label: Sakin MG38 EXT (Negev NG7)
ThingDef.description: A complex light machine gun design with mechanisms allowing a low but consistent firerate to control recoil, paired with a seemingly limitless ammo reservoir for situations requiring a continuous, non-stop output. EXT stands for Extended Mag. Unlike an IAR, you don't have to build a system around the gun for more shots. The gun is already equipped for more. This variant adds more bullets fired at the cost or range and accuracy.

ThingDef.label: Sakin MG38 SNP (Negev NG7)
ThingDef.description: A complex light machine gun design with mechanisms allowing a low but consistent firerate to control recoil, paired with a seemingly limitless ammo reservoir for situations requiring a continuous, non-stop output. SNP stands for Sniper. Not everyone is a Marksman but that doesn't stop someone from trying. Lowering the ammo count for more better LR accuracy and range.

ThingDef.label: HCR-56 (AUG HBAR)
ThingDef.description: A heavy-barrel variant of the well-known bullpup rifle platform, configured to provide sustained automatic fire in a compact form. The reinforced barrel and support components improve heat resistance and stability, allowing it to function effectively in a light support role while retaining the platform’s compact handling.

ThingDef.label: HCR-56 INF (AUG HBAR)
ThingDef.description: A heavy-barrel variant of the well-known bullpup rifle platform, configured to provide sustained automatic fire in a compact form. The reinforced barrel and support components improve heat resistance and stability, allowing it to function effectively in a light support role while retaining the platform’s compact handling. INF stands for Infantry. This variant represents the standardise weapon used by a soldier. It's good in build and good at medium to long range.

ThingDef.label: HCR-56 CQC (AUG HBAR)
ThingDef.description: A heavy-barrel variant of the well-known bullpup rifle platform, configured to provide sustained automatic fire in a compact form. The reinforced barrel and support components improve heat resistance and stability, allowing it to function effectively in a light support role while retaining the platform’s compact handling. CQC stands for Close Quaters Combat. This variant is designed for upclose encounters like room clearing and urban warfare. Sacrifices long range for better short range accuracy.

ThingDef.label: HCR-56 SF (AUG HBAR)
ThingDef.description: A heavy-barrel variant of the well-known bullpup rifle platform, configured to provide sustained automatic fire in a compact form. The reinforced barrel and support components improve heat resistance and stability, allowing it to function effectively in a light support role while retaining the platform’s compact handling. SF stands for Special Forces. This variant is equipped with a supressor and more quality teched gear. Perfect for anyone who needs to be stealthy and efficient.

ThingDef.label: HCR-56 THM (AUG HBAR)
ThingDef.description: A heavy-barrel variant of the well-known bullpup rifle platform, configured to provide sustained automatic fire in a compact form. The reinforced barrel and support components improve heat resistance and stability, allowing it to function effectively in a light support role while retaining the platform’s compact handling. THM stands for Thermals. This variant is equipped with a Thermal Scope. Being able to see your target lit up like a Christmas Tree makes target acquisition easier. However, when the target gets closer it becomes harder to see.

ThingDef.label: HCR-56 EXT (AUG HBAR)
ThingDef.description: A heavy-barrel variant of the well-known bullpup rifle platform, configured to provide sustained automatic fire in a compact form. The reinforced barrel and support components improve heat resistance and stability, allowing it to function effectively in a light support role while retaining the platform’s compact handling.EXT stands for Extended Mag. Unlike an IAR, you don't have to build a system around the gun for more shots. The gun is already equipped for more. This variant adds more bullets fired at the cost or range and accuracy.

ThingDef.label: HCR-56 SNP (AUG HBAR)
ThingDef.description: A heavy-barrel variant of the well-known bullpup rifle platform, configured to provide sustained automatic fire in a compact form. The reinforced barrel and support components improve heat resistance and stability, allowing it to function effectively in a light support role while retaining the platform’s compact handling. SNP stands for Sniper. Not everyone is a Marksman but that doesn't stop someone from trying. Lowering the ammo count for more better LR accuracy and range.

ThingDef.label: 556 Icarus (Fightlite MCR)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed light machine gun built on a modular rifle platform, combining high ammunition capacity with the ergonomics of a carbine-style layout. Its design allows sustained automatic fire while remaining lighter and more adaptable than traditional machine guns, supporting mobile fire support roles.

ThingDef.label: 556 Icarus INF (Fightlite MCR)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed light machine gun built on a modular rifle platform, combining high ammunition capacity with the ergonomics of a carbine-style layout. Its design allows sustained automatic fire while remaining lighter and more adaptable than traditional machine guns, supporting mobile fire support roles. INF stands for Infantry. This variant represents the standardise weapon used by a soldier. It's good in build and good at medium to long range.

ThingDef.label: 556 Icarus CQC (Fightlite MCR)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed light machine gun built on a modular rifle platform, combining high ammunition capacity with the ergonomics of a carbine-style layout. Its design allows sustained automatic fire while remaining lighter and more adaptable than traditional machine guns, supporting mobile fire support roles. CQC stands for Close Quaters Combat. This variant is designed for upclose encounters like room clearing and urban warfare. Sacrifices long range for better short range accuracy.

ThingDef.label: 556 Icarus SF (Fightlite MCR)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed light machine gun built on a modular rifle platform, combining high ammunition capacity with the ergonomics of a carbine-style layout. Its design allows sustained automatic fire while remaining lighter and more adaptable than traditional machine guns, supporting mobile fire support roles. SF stands for Special Forces. This variant is equipped with a supressor and more quality teched gear. Perfect for anyone who needs to be stealthy and efficient.

ThingDef.label: 556 Icarus THM (Fightlite MCR)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed light machine gun built on a modular rifle platform, combining high ammunition capacity with the ergonomics of a carbine-style layout. Its design allows sustained automatic fire while remaining lighter and more adaptable than traditional machine guns, supporting mobile fire support roles. THM stands for Thermals. This variant is equipped with a Thermal Scope. Being able to see your target lit up like a Christmas Tree makes target acquisition easier. However, when the target gets closer it becomes harder to see.

ThingDef.label: 556 Icarus EXT (Fightlite MCR)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed light machine gun built on a modular rifle platform, combining high ammunition capacity with the ergonomics of a carbine-style layout. Its design allows sustained automatic fire while remaining lighter and more adaptable than traditional machine guns, supporting mobile fire support roles.EXT stands for Extended Mag. Unlike an IAR, you don't have to build a system around the gun for more shots. The gun is already equipped for more. This variant adds more bullets fired at the cost or range and accuracy.

ThingDef.label: 556 Icarus SNP (Fightlite MCR)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed light machine gun built on a modular rifle platform, combining high ammunition capacity with the ergonomics of a carbine-style layout. Its design allows sustained automatic fire while remaining lighter and more adaptable than traditional machine guns, supporting mobile fire support roles. SNP stands for Sniper. Not everyone is a Marksman but that doesn't stop someone from trying. Lowering the ammo count for more better LR accuracy and range.

ThingDef.label: RAAL MG (MG338)
ThingDef.description: A modern machine gun developed to bridge the gap between traditional medium and heavy machine guns. Chambered for a high-power cartridge and built with advanced materials, it delivers extended effective range and strong penetration while remaining portable enough for mobile fire support roles.

ThingDef.label: RAAL MG INF (MG338)
ThingDef.description: A modern machine gun developed to bridge the gap between traditional medium and heavy machine guns. Chambered for a high-power cartridge and built with advanced materials, it delivers extended effective range and strong penetration while remaining portable enough for mobile fire support roles. INF stands for Infantry. This variant represents the standardise weapon used by a soldier. It's good in build and good at medium to long range.

ThingDef.label: RAAL MG CQC (MG338)
ThingDef.description: A modern machine gun developed to bridge the gap between traditional medium and heavy machine guns. Chambered for a high-power cartridge and built with advanced materials, it delivers extended effective range and strong penetration while remaining portable enough for mobile fire support roles. CQC stands for Close Quaters Combat. This variant is designed for upclose encounters like room clearing and urban warfare. Sacrifices long range for better short range accuracy.

ThingDef.label: RAAL MG SF (MG338)
ThingDef.description: A modern machine gun developed to bridge the gap between traditional medium and heavy machine guns. Chambered for a high-power cartridge and built with advanced materials, it delivers extended effective range and strong penetration while remaining portable enough for mobile fire support roles. SF stands for Special Forces. This variant is equipped with a supressor and more quality teched gear. Perfect for anyone who needs to be stealthy and efficient.

ThingDef.label: RAAL MG THM (MG338)
ThingDef.description: A modern machine gun developed to bridge the gap between traditional medium and heavy machine guns. Chambered for a high-power cartridge and built with advanced materials, it delivers extended effective range and strong penetration while remaining portable enough for mobile fire support roles. THM stands for Thermals. This variant is equipped with a Thermal Scope. Being able to see your target lit up like a Christmas Tree makes target acquisition easier. However, when the target gets closer it becomes harder to see.

ThingDef.label: RAAL MG EXT (MG338)
ThingDef.description: A modern machine gun developed to bridge the gap between traditional medium and heavy machine guns. Chambered for a high-power cartridge and built with advanced materials, it delivers extended effective range and strong penetration while remaining portable enough for mobile fire support roles.EXT stands for Extended Mag. Unlike an IAR, you don't have to build a system around the gun for more shots. The gun is already equipped for more. This variant adds more bullets fired at the cost or range and accuracy.

ThingDef.label: RAAL MG SNP (MG338)
ThingDef.description: A modern machine gun developed to bridge the gap between traditional medium and heavy machine guns. Chambered for a high-power cartridge and built with advanced materials, it delivers extended effective range and strong penetration while remaining portable enough for mobile fire support roles. SNP stands for Sniper. Not everyone is a Marksman but that doesn't stop someone from trying. Lowering the ammo count for more better LR accuracy and range.

ThingDef.label: RPK
ThingDef.description: A squad support weapon derived from a classic rifle platform, adapted with a longer barrel and reinforced components to sustain automatic fire. Its design provides increased stability and ammunition capacity while remaining practical for mobile infantry support.

ThingDef.label: RPK INF
ThingDef.description: A squad support weapon derived from a classic rifle platform, adapted with a longer barrel and reinforced components to sustain automatic fire. Its design provides increased stability and ammunition capacity while remaining practical for mobile infantry support. INF stands for Infantry. This variant represents the standardise weapon used by a soldier. It's good in build and good at medium to long range.

ThingDef.label: RPK CQC
ThingDef.description: A squad support weapon derived from a classic rifle platform, adapted with a longer barrel and reinforced components to sustain automatic fire. Its design provides increased stability and ammunition capacity while remaining practical for mobile infantry support. CQC stands for Close Quaters Combat. This variant is designed for upclose encounters like room clearing and urban warfare. Sacrifices long range for better short range accuracy.

ThingDef.label: RPK SF
ThingDef.description: A squad support weapon derived from a classic rifle platform, adapted with a longer barrel and reinforced components to sustain automatic fire. Its design provides increased stability and ammunition capacity while remaining practical for mobile infantry support. SF stands for Special Forces. This variant is equipped with a supressor and more quality teched gear. Perfect for anyone who needs to be stealthy and efficient.

ThingDef.label: RPK THM
ThingDef.description: A squad support weapon derived from a classic rifle platform, adapted with a longer barrel and reinforced components to sustain automatic fire. Its design provides increased stability and ammunition capacity while remaining practical for mobile infantry support. THM stands for Thermals. This variant is equipped with a Thermal Scope. Being able to see your target lit up like a Christmas Tree makes target acquisition easier. However, when the target gets closer it becomes harder to see.

ThingDef.label: RPK EXT
ThingDef.description: A squad support weapon derived from a classic rifle platform, adapted with a longer barrel and reinforced components to sustain automatic fire. Its design provides increased stability and ammunition capacity while remaining practical for mobile infantry support.EXT stands for Extended Mag. Unlike an IAR, you don't have to build a system around the gun for more shots. The gun is already equipped for more. This variant adds more bullets fired at the cost or range and accuracy.

ThingDef.label: RPKM
ThingDef.description: A squad support weapon derived from a classic rifle platform, adapted with a longer barrel and reinforced components to sustain automatic fire. Its design provides increased stability and ammunition capacity while remaining practical for mobile infantry support.

ThingDef.label: RAPP-H (HK21)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed machine gun built around a roller-delayed operating system, designed to deliver sustained automatic fire from a relatively compact platform. Its configuration supports continuous fire and adaptable feeding systems, allowing it to serve effectively in a squad support role.

ThingDef.label: RAPP-H INF(HK21)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed machine gun built around a roller-delayed operating system, designed to deliver sustained automatic fire from a relatively compact platform. Its configuration supports continuous fire and adaptable feeding systems, allowing it to serve effectively in a squad support role. INF stands for Infantry. This variant represents the standardise weapon used by a soldier. It's good in build and good at medium to long range.

ThingDef.label: RAPP-H CQC(HK21)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed machine gun built around a roller-delayed operating system, designed to deliver sustained automatic fire from a relatively compact platform. Its configuration supports continuous fire and adaptable feeding systems, allowing it to serve effectively in a squad support role. CQC stands for Close Quaters Combat. This variant is designed for upclose encounters like room clearing and urban warfare. Sacrifices long range for better short range accuracy.

ThingDef.label: RAPP-H SF(HK21)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed machine gun built around a roller-delayed operating system, designed to deliver sustained automatic fire from a relatively compact platform. Its configuration supports continuous fire and adaptable feeding systems, allowing it to serve effectively in a squad support role. SF stands for Special Forces. This variant is equipped with a supressor and more quality teched gear. Perfect for anyone who needs to be stealthy and efficient.

ThingDef.label: RAPP-H THM(HK21)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed machine gun built around a roller-delayed operating system, designed to deliver sustained automatic fire from a relatively compact platform. Its configuration supports continuous fire and adaptable feeding systems, allowing it to serve effectively in a squad support role. THM stands for Thermals. This variant is equipped with a Thermal Scope. Being able to see your target lit up like a Christmas Tree makes target acquisition easier. However, when the target gets closer it becomes harder to see.

ThingDef.label: RAPP-H EXT(HK21)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed machine gun built around a roller-delayed operating system, designed to deliver sustained automatic fire from a relatively compact platform. Its configuration supports continuous fire and adaptable feeding systems, allowing it to serve effectively in a squad support role.EXT stands for Extended Mag. Unlike an IAR, you don't have to build a system around the gun for more shots. The gun is already equipped for more. This variant adds more bullets fired at the cost or range and accuracy.

ThingDef.label: RAPP-H SNP(HK21)
ThingDef.description: A belt-fed machine gun built around a roller-delayed operating system, designed to deliver sustained automatic fire from a relatively compact platform. Its configuration supports continuous fire and adaptable feeding systems, allowing it to serve effectively in a squad support role. SNP stands for Sniper. Not everyone is a Marksman but that doesn't stop someone from trying. Lowering the ammo count for more better LR accuracy and range.
